item.title: Enjoy this dose of wholesome. It was one of the few good things that came out of the tire fire that was 2020.

item.title: Time for the unhinged XL Thursday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
*window
not wrong?
mmm hmm
i have no
what are you doing, step can!!??!
but really who hasn't done this at least once
yes
finally
what else should someone draw? i know what happened, good enough
ok then
anyone know where this station is?
looks like a reticulated python
*sigh* i'm in..
sorry
wait what
can confirm
ahh, that old chestnut
that’s it for me. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

Today's your lucky day coz @hetherweaversmagic has just the boy for you: https://imgur.com/gallery/FiAc94m And he really could use a good human right about now.

item.title: Images of Note: Jinux
item.description:
GET THAT VIT D!
Hey Cishets, you okay?
BILLIONS MUST TRY!
Ok but a Moorland like in Scotland is where you meet the Devil or perhaps make a speech about how it's shite being Scottish.
I feel personally attacked
HELL YEAH MINT
Why does this make sense
I love the whole "Mechanicus loves toasters" thing
Why tho
I love how this happened in Glasgow, I could've gotten a train and watched this unfold live.
This is why he puts St. Peter on the Gate.
Jinux. Jux if you will. Jnu/Jinux if you are so inclined.
:(
ALL HAIL
:D
Never change, Glasgow
creechur
owo
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: "If he Pledges, he Pledges."
item.description: With everything going on this week, you may have missed the legendary actor and philanthropist, Dolph Lundgren, receiving his official citizenship to the United States. Well earned, if I do say so myself.

item.title: Your shy gal made some shy guys!
item.description:
3D printed some red and green Shy Guys!
Love the dopey little face option
Also doubles as a storage container! Designed by Pla-n-pa. Printed on an FLsunV400 Sadly they are not $11.99

item.title: Busting a music myth about a song you don't know from a band you've never heard of
item.description:
See that young fella in the front, with his sleeveless shirt tucked into his rolled up denim cutoffs? That's a wee baby Trent Reznor (of Nine Inch Nails fame). Next to him is Al Jourgensen, of many things, but best known for pioneering industrial rock band Ministry. One of Al's "many things" was a little side project called 1000 Homo DJs (hey I'm just the OP, I don't name the bands, but I'll drop the history of that name at the end of this post) and Trent was sorta... kinda... part of that. And it was scandalous! Here's the story...
Trent was still pretty early in his career, and jut starting to move into the darker dirtier sound, after years with synth pop bands like Exotic Birds and Slam Bamboo (above). Al & Ministry were already pretty well established in that arena, and "industrial rock" as a genre was still shaping itself. So they teamed up for a cover of the Black Sabbath song "Supernaut" where Trent did the vocals, and here's where the myth starts.
What's 100% certain: -Trent sang the initial version of Supernaut without permission from his label -His label (TVT) didn't like that What's disputed: -Al then sang a new vocal track to replace Trent's The Myth: Industrial rockers, being what they were, gave the finger to the label and just altered Trent's vocals to sound different, thereby clearing the hurdle without actually "giving in".
Here's the thing though... that didn't happen. Play the above a couple times, listening especially to the last word of each line - sky and fly. Trent's version (bottom) he splits the words into three parts - sky-y-y and fly-y-y. The top track, labeled as Al, the words are in two parts - sky-y and fly-y. I went through the whole song bit by bit, and there are many such differences, some of them even more noticeable. Which means it's 100% certain to be a different vocal recording. So... no reason at all to believe it's Trent on both. Even the Wikipedia entry on the band lists this myth as a possibility but... sorry all. One is Trent, one is Al, just like they told the labels. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- As promised: In a 2004 interview, Jourgensen explained that when he played demos for Wax Trax! co-owner Jim Nash, the latter replied, "No one's gonna buy this. It'll take one thousand homo DJs to play this for one person to buy it." - Wikipedia If you're into deep musical dives, this one was fun to write https://imgur.com/gallery/OYVKYbD Edit: As long as I'm on FP, might as well plug the band's official Bandcamp page. https://ministryband.bandcamp.com/album/1000-homo-djs-supernaut
item.dc:creator: Magnebro
